statement_text: It intentionally mixes `must`, `must not`, `required to`, prohibited forms, and a small number of `shall` statements.
- **2.1.1 RC-CYB-01.** Privileged administrators must authenticate through the approved mission identity provider using phishing-resistant multifactor credentials.
- **2.1.2 RC-CYB-02.** Shared maintenance accounts must not be used for routine administration of the fielded relay node.
- **2.1.3 RC-CYB-03.** All support personnel are required to complete role-specific access recertification every 180 days.
- **2.1.4 RC-CYB-04.** Field support laptops may not store reusable administrative passwords in browser caches or unmanaged key stores.
- **2.1.4 RC-CYB-04.** Field support laptops may not store reusable administrative passwords in browser caches or unmanaged key stores.
- **2.1.5 RC-CYB-05.** The mission support enclave shall present operators with a lockout warning before a credential reaches its expiration threshold.
- **2.1.6 RC-CYB-06.** Break-glass credentials are prohibited from routine operational use and are required to be escrowed in the sealed emergency access package.
- **2.1.7 RC-CYB-07.** Role assignments are required to be reviewed whenever a user changes operational function, duty location, or employing organization.
- **2.1.8 RC-CYB-08.** Temporary administrator sessions must not remain active after completion of the approved maintenance window.
- **3.2.1 RC-CYB-09.** Delivered software packages must include provenance metadata sufficient to identify build pipeline, source revision, and signing identity.
- **3.2.2 RC-CYB-10.** Unsigned container images must not be loaded into the depot integration environment.
- **3.2.2 RC-CYB-10.** Unsigned container images must not be loaded into the depot integration environment.
- **3.2.3 RC-CYB-11.** Third-party software components are required to be listed in the delivered software bill of materials together with version and license information.
- **3.2.4 RC-CYB-12.** Components with known critical vulnerabilities may not remain in an operational baseline once an approved remediation package is available.
- **3.2.5 RC-CYB-13.** The contractor shall identify all supplier dependencies that can affect patch lead time or vulnerability response timing.
- **3.2.6 RC-CYB-14.** Portable media used for software transfer must be scanned in the staging environment before connection to any mission-support system.
- **3.2.7 RC-CYB-15.** Supplier security attestations are required to identify both current controls and any unresolved corrective actions that could affect delivered software.
- **3.2.8 RC-CYB-16.** Development repositories may not rely on public package mirrors during controlled release builds unless those mirrors are routed through the approved inspection gateway.
- **4.3.1 RC-CYB-17.** Security-relevant event records are required to retain node identity, user identity, timestamp, action outcome, and configuration state change details.
- **4.3.2 RC-CYB-18.** Audit records must not be editable by the same role that generated the event.
- **4.3.3 RC-CYB-19.** The fielded node is prohibited from forwarding high-volume debug traffic over constrained mission links during normal operations.
- **4.3.4 RC-CYB-20.** Incident triage teams are required to preserve volatile evidence before initiating a restore action when mission conditions allow.
- **4.3.5 RC-CYB-21.** The relay node shall preserve locally queued cybersecurity events for upload after backhaul restoration.
- **4.3.6 RC-CYB-22.** Maintenance users may not disable audit generation in order to reduce storage consumption during expeditionary operations.
- **4.3.6 RC-CYB-22.** Maintenance users may not disable audit generation in order to reduce storage consumption during expeditionary operations.
- **4.3.7 RC-CYB-23.** Incident tickets are required to record whether mission impact, data exposure, or assurance degradation occurred during the observed event.
- **4.3.8 RC-CYB-24.** Forensic exports must not omit hash values, baseline identifiers, or operator-observed symptoms that can affect reconstruction of the event timeline.
- **5.4.1 RC-CYB-25.** Accreditation support packages are required to identify residual risk assumptions that depend on external enclaves or shared services.
- **5.4.2 RC-CYB-26.** Waiver requests must not be used to defer remediation of a known exploit path when an approved corrective action already exists.
- **5.4.3 RC-CYB-27.** The program shall maintain a living cyber dependency map for fielded hardware, software, and support-tool components.
- **5.4.4 RC-CYB-28.** Sustainment organizations are required to document how emergency updates are reviewed, approved, distributed, and verified.
- **5.4.5 RC-CYB-29.** Evidence repositories may not store exported mission data longer than the retention period approved for the associated investigation or audit purpose.
- **5.4.6 RC-CYB-30.** Mission rehearsal images must be clearly labeled and are required to remain segregated from operational release candidates.
The external power inlet shall mate with the approved expeditionary generator connector set without requiring an adapter harness.
The shelter power interface shall indicate phase loss, grounding fault status, and protective-device state on the local service panel.
The interface shall tolerate hot reconnect after controlled shutdown without damaging connected mission equipment.
The power quality monitor shall sample voltage and frequency once per second and shall timestamp each out-of-family event.
The power quality monitor shall sample voltage and frequency once per second and shall timestamp each out-of-family event.
The shelter interface shall not backfeed the generator source during transient changeover to internal reserve power.
The contractor is required to label all field-replaceable power-interface assemblies with connector family, mating torque, and inspection interval information.
The management interface shall support role-based sessions for operator, maintainer, and cybersecurity officer profiles.
The interface shall reject malformed control transactions and shall record the rejection event with user and node context.
The management gateway shall provide a read-only health summary endpoint for mission command dashboards.
Control channels may not share the same virtual routing domain as externally exposed user-data traffic.
Maintenance consoles are required to present software baseline and serial-number information prior to allowing update actions.
The management client shall display an explicit warning before any action that can terminate active traffic forwarding.
The protected data interface shall support the threshold throughput profile defined by the Raven Crest network baseline.
The waveform gateway interface shall preserve sequence ordering for priority traffic classes during backhaul transition events.
The timing input interface shall accept the approved GNSS reference signal and shall hold internal timing within threshold limits after reference loss.
The external data interface shall tag audit exports with node identity, enclosure identity, and coordinated timestamp of origin.
Transit buffers shall be cleared after mission termination and shall not remain accessible through maintenance browsing actions.
The contractor shall identify any interface timing assumptions that are external to the Raven Crest delivered baseline.
Built-in-test output shall identify the affected replaceable assembly, observed fault class, and recommended immediate action.
Maintenance ports shall be physically accessible without removal of permanently installed cabling bundles.
The support-data interface shall permit export of maintenance history and shall prevent silent truncation of the exported file.
Service access points must not expose energized conductors during organizational maintenance tasks.
Support laptops are prohibited from auto-mounting removable storage that lacks the approved scan signature.
The maintainer interface shall provide a confirmation message after successful log archive completion.
The shelter tie-down interface shall match the approved tactical trailer restraint geometry.
Lift points shall accommodate the approved spreader-bar arrangement and shall be marked with serialized inspection information.
Cable entry glands shall preserve the environmental seal after repeated approved field-service actions.
The installation interface shall permit antenna mast connection using common support tools only.
Temporary transport covers are required to remain captive or otherwise tethered to the protected assembly during removal.
Packaging and transport labels shall use the program-defined item nomenclature and configuration revision identifiers.
The qualification instrumentation interface shall expose voltage, current, thermal, and timing observables needed for the approved event set.
Safety interlocks shall remain visible to the test conductor and maintenance observer during energized troubleshooting actions.
The event setup interface shall identify whether protective covers, warning barriers, and emergency stop controls are installed before power application.
Temporary test leads may not obscure required labels or maintenance disconnect points during event execution.
The contractor shall provide a cross-reference between interface clauses and the planned verification event setup sheets.
Portable instrumentation is required to be bonded and labeled in accordance with the approved site safety practice before connection to the shelter.
The Contractor shall maintain an integrated task schedule showing design, procurement, fabrication, qualification, and delivery events at a level sufficient for government surveillance.
The Contractor shall participate in weekly technical interchange meetings and monthly program management reviews using the government-provided agenda template.
The Contractor shall identify schedule variance drivers greater than five working days and provide recovery actions in the next monthly report.
The Contractor shall submit an updated open-issues log before each formal review event.

The Contractor shall support System Requirements Review, Preliminary Design Review, Critical Design Review, and Production Readiness Review with required presentation material and action-item closure evidence.
The Contractor shall coordinate all drawing releases through the Silver Peak Mission Systems configuration lead.
The Contractor shall design the transport and integration hardware so that it supports the approved interface baseline without unauthorized substitution of controlled materials or finishes.
The Contractor shall fabricate qualification and production-representative article sets using processes approved by the program materials board.
The Contractor shall maintain manufacturing travelers for each serialized assembly and shall preserve those travelers for not less than seven years after final delivery.
The Contractor shall support fit-check, thermal, vibration, and transport demonstration events at the government integration site.
The Contractor shall document all deviations, waivers, and nonconformances in the approved discrepancy reporting format.
The Contractor shall not substitute connector families, structural adhesives, or corrosion-control treatments without prior written approval.
The Contractor shall implement access controls for all digital design repositories used to store Raven Crest technical data.
The Contractor shall ensure that all externally delivered digital files are malware scanned prior to transmittal.
The Contractor shall provide a monthly quality metrics summary covering defect escapes, rework hours, and supplier-caused material shortfalls.
The Contractor shall preserve configuration status accounting for part numbers, drawing revisions, and serialized hardware deliveries.
The Contractor shall report any suspected compromise of government-furnished technical data within 24 hours of discovery.
The Contractor shall segregate Raven Crest controlled technical data from unrelated commercial program data in shared engineering environments.
The Contractor shall package all deliverable hardware in accordance with the approved preservation and packing instructions.
The Contractor shall deliver first article hardware no later than 28 calendar days before the start of the qualification event window.
The Contractor shall provide two on-site support representatives during first article installation and checkout.
The Contractor shall provide two on-site support representatives during first article installation and checkout.
The Contractor shall submit recommended spare parts, special tools, and depot-only repair candidates as part of the provisioning support package.
The Contractor shall train designated government maintainers on installation, removal, inspection, and basic fault isolation tasks.
The Contractor shall support root-cause assessment for any hardware-attributed field failure reported during the warranty period.
The Contractor shall identify every deliverable drawing, parts list, process note, and software utility by controlled revision identifier in the monthly configuration status report.
The Contractor shall flag any contractor-owned background data proposed for use in a deliverable item and shall identify the intended license treatment before delivery.
The Contractor shall provide a parts obsolescence watch list with each quarterly status package.
The Contractor shall provide a parts obsolescence watch list with each quarterly status package.
The Contractor shall maintain traceability between procurement records, receiving inspection records, and serialized assembly identifiers.
The Contractor shall preserve redline build changes and shall reconcile those changes into released data prior to lot acceptance.
The Contractor shall deliver native digital source files for CDRL items identified as editable government products.
The Contractor shall provide a transition brief before the start of full-rate production support.
The Contractor shall maintain an active warranty response point of contact for the duration specified in the subcontract.
The Contractor shall deliver a final lessons-learned package summarizing manufacturing escapes, recurring support burdens, and recommended process improvements.
The Contractor shall support one closeout review to confirm completion status for all open technical and data-delivery actions.
The Contractor shall retain tooling calibration and acceptance records until formal closeout direction is received.
The Contractor shall identify any residual support dependencies that would affect future competition for sustainment or redesign work.
Narrative discussion is included so that extracted shall statements remain surrounded by realistic engineering prose rather than isolated checklist fragments.
The system shall establish full relay service within 18 minutes of arrival at the emplacement site when operated by a four-person crew using the issued setup kit.
The system shall sustain not less than 96 simultaneous secure voice paths while carrying mission command data on the same backhaul segment.
The system shall route protected IP traffic between line-of-sight, satellite, and terrestrial backhaul paths without operator re-entry of session information.
The system shall maintain end-to-end availability of 0.995 across a 72-hour mission profile when all required preventive maintenance actions are performed at the specified intervals.
The system shall provide a mission planning load package that can be transferred from removable media and shall reject a load package whose signature validation fails.
The system shall preserve current traffic sessions for at least 45 seconds during autonomous backhaul failover.
The system shall display relay-link health, antenna pointing status, and crypto-fill age on a single operator overview screen.
The system shall display relay-link health, antenna pointing status, and crypto-fill age on a single operator overview screen.
The system shall support two geographically separated remote operator sessions without degrading mission traffic throughput below the threshold value.
The system shall archive configuration changes, alarm acknowledgements, and operator role changes in a mission audit file that can be exported after shutdown.
The system shall continue mission traffic forwarding after the loss of any single non-hostile cooling fan and shall annunciate the failed assembly within 10 seconds.
The system shall provide an alternate low-signature operating mode that reduces acoustic output below 48 dBA at 5 meters while sustaining priority command traffic.
Narrative discussion is included so that extracted shall statements remain surrounded by realistic engineering prose rather than isolated checklist fragments.
The system shall accept primary input power from 208 VAC three-phase service and from the approved expeditionary generator interface without rewiring at the shelter bulkhead.
The system shall tolerate input voltage excursions of plus or minus 10 percent without mission interruption or component damage.
The system shall tolerate input voltage excursions of plus or minus 10 percent without mission interruption or component damage.
The system shall maintain internal equipment inlet temperatures within vendor limits at an ambient temperature of 49 C and a solar loading condition representative of desert emplacement.
The system shall automatically shed non-mission-essential convenience loads before shedding protected communications loads.
The system shall not expose operator touch surfaces to temperatures greater than 45 C during sustained hot-day operation.
The environmental control assembly shall recover the shelter to nominal operating temperature within 30 minutes after a hot restart sequence.
The shelter power distribution unit shall isolate a branch fault to the affected branch and shall preserve service to all remaining protected loads.
The system shall withstand transport vibration, rain intrusion, blowing dust, salt fog, and fungal exposure in accordance with the tailored environmental profile in Appendix A.
The system shall provide automatic orderly shutdown logic when available fuel, battery reserve, and thermal capacity indicate that continued operation would risk data corruption.
The system shall report hourly fuel burn, internal temperature extrema, and load utilization for transfer into the mission support log.
The system shall be capable of 24-hour unattended environmental control when a remote watch station is connected to the maintenance network.
Narrative discussion is included so that extracted shall statements remain surrounded by realistic engineering prose rather than isolated checklist fragments.
The system shall support routing for IPv4 and IPv6 mission enclaves across the protected and coalition enclaves defined in the network architecture baseline.
The system shall prioritize fires, air defense, and theater logistics traffic classes in the order specified by the mission quality-of-service profile.
The system shall encrypt all external user-data paths end to end and shall reject unauthenticated control messages presented on the management interface.
The system shall synchronize internal timing to the approved GNSS reference and shall hold timing accuracy within 250 microseconds for not less than 30 minutes after GNSS loss.
The system shall expose an interface status summary through SNMPv3 and through the local maintenance dashboard using the approved object set.
The system shall sanitize temporary mission buffers during controlled shutdown and after completion of an operator-requested purge action.
The system shall support over-the-air rekey while mission traffic remains active on unaffected channels.
The system shall label all exported audit, alert, and health messages with the node identifier, enclosure identifier, and coordinated timestamp of origin.
The system shall continue internal message routing when one non-primary switch module is removed for replacement in accordance with the approved maintenance procedure.
The system shall provide deterministic interface timing for external waveform gateways at the threshold rate defined in Interface Annex C.
The system shall enforce protocol and port allow lists on all external management connections.
Narrative discussion is included so that extracted shall statements remain surrounded by realistic engineering prose rather than isolated checklist fragments.
The system shall be operable by a crew of four with no task requiring more than two personnel lifts over 35 pounds.
The operator workstation shall present alarm text in plain language, shall identify the affected assembly, and shall display the recommended immediate action on the same screen.
The system shall include on-platform help for startup, fault isolation, rekey, and controlled shutdown tasks.
The system shall permit replacement of the primary network switch, crypto module, power supply tray, and environmental control controller using common support tools only.
The mean time to restore any line-replaceable unit designated as mission critical shall not exceed 30 minutes at the organizational maintenance level.
The system shall log all maintenance actions with part number, serial number, operator identifier, and time of completion.
The system shall provide embedded diagnostics that isolate not less than 90 percent of critical faults to a single line-replaceable unit.
The system shall support training mode playback using recorded alarm and status scenarios without placing live mission circuits at risk.
The system shall preserve all exported maintenance logs and shall prevent overwriting of the most recent 45 days of history until an explicit archive action is completed.
The system shall include lifting, tie-down, and access provisions that support helicopter sling transport and standard tactical trailer movement.
The contractor-furnished support package shall include illustrated parts data, wiring diagrams, and maintenance task narratives aligned to the delivered configuration baseline.
Narrative discussion is included so that extracted shall statements remain surrounded by realistic engineering prose rather than isolated checklist fragments.
The system shall authenticate all privileged users with multifactor credentials approved for the mission enclave.
The system shall not permit factory-default credentials to remain active after acceptance test completion.
The system shall record all successful and failed privileged logins, key management actions, and security policy changes in tamper-evident audit records.
The system shall apply software images only after signature verification against the approved release manifest.
The system shall prevent execution of unapproved removable-media autorun content on any mission or maintenance workstation.
The system shall support role-based administration for operators, maintainers, information assurance officers, and depot sustainment users.
The system shall generate a cybersecurity posture summary that identifies patch level, signature-set currency, and unresolved high-severity findings.
The system shall isolate maintenance traffic from mission traffic using separate logical domains and separately enforced policy sets.
The system shall protect stored mission keys at rest using hardware-backed cryptographic storage.
The system shall transfer security-relevant logs to the designated collector when reach-back service is available and shall queue those logs locally when reach-back is unavailable.
The system shall support formal configuration baseline comparison against the approved release package prior to mission start.
Narrative discussion is included so that extracted shall statements remain surrounded by realistic engineering prose rather than isolated checklist fragments.
The system shall use only connector finishes, cable jackets, and corrosion-control materials approved by the program materials board.
The system shall not require a unique transport frame beyond the approved tactical trailer and shelter skid arrangement.
The system shall provide safety markings, lockout points, and cautions consistent with the program hazard analysis and operator manual.
The system shall support shipment with the on-system spare kit, operator kit, and tool roll in the packaged configuration defined by Section 5.
The delivered baseline shall include a serialized asset list, software bill of materials, and configuration item cross-reference for each production unit.
The system shall remain interoperable with the fielded Raven Crest command post gateway set through Increment 2 transition.
The system shall not depend on cloud-hosted services for local mission continuity functions.
The system shall not depend on cloud-hosted services for local mission continuity functions.
The system shall provide a calibration and inspection interval table for assemblies whose drift could affect timing, power quality, or antenna pointing performance.
The program documentation set shall identify all contractor-developed interface control data required to support future competition for sustainment activities.
The system shall support storage for 12 months in warehouse conditions without requiring disassembly of sealed mission equipment.
The system shall include warning labels and access barriers sufficient to prevent inadvertent contact with energized high-voltage points during organizational maintenance.
Several requirements in Section 3 are tighter than commercial norms because the node must operate under expeditionary thermal and mission continuity constraints.
Each verification row shall reference exactly one governing requirement identifier and one primary verification method.
Derived verification notes shall be clearly marked as supporting rationale rather than acceptance criteria.
Cross-reference tables shall retain prior-row identifiers when a requirement is reallocated to a lower configuration item.
Verification analysts shall identify any requirement statement that contains multiple mandatory operators and shall flag the row for quality review before procedure release.
Imported requirement rows shall preserve source document title, baseline revision, and clause number exactly as released in the controlling baseline.
Timing and throughput requirements shall be mapped to test or demonstration methods unless the program verification lead approves an analysis substitute.
Physical interface fit, labeling, and packaging characteristics shall be mapped to examination when direct inspection provides objective evidence.
Cybersecurity evidence packages are required to identify the exact software baseline, scan content, and unresolved finding posture used to support the assessment.
Requirements that depend on mission continuity under fault conditions shall be traced to event sequences that demonstrate fault injection and recovery.
Verification planners shall document any assumption that depends on facility limitations, instrumentation constraints, or approved mission-profile tailoring.
When a requirement references external interface timing, the verification package shall identify the companion interface clause or annex used for the event setup.
The verification matrix shall preserve the source document title, clause number, and baseline revision for each imported requirement identifier.
location: Clause 4 · Review Flow and Change Management · Offset 54

statement_text: Review comments are required to record whether a disputed row concerns requirement wording, allocation, verification scope, or evidence sufficiency.
location: Clause 4 · Review Flow and Change Management · Offset 216

statement_text: Closed review comments may not be deleted from the released verification record; they are required to remain visible for audit purposes.
location: Clause 4 · Review Flow and Change Management · Offset 382

statement_text: Baseline updates shall trigger a traceability impact review before legacy procedures are reused for a new qualification event.
location: Clause 4 · Review Flow and Change Management · Offset 536

statement_text: The verification lead shall record the disposition of every requirement row affected by a change package, waiver, or approved tailoring action.
location: Clause 4 · Review Flow and Change Management · Offset 680

statement_text: Objective evidence packages shall identify event configuration, witness roles, instrumentation set, and discrepancy status for the associated verification result.
location: Clause 5 · Evidence Packaging and Delivery · Offset 52

statement_text: Exported evidence bundles are required to retain hash values, source filenames, and generation timestamps for each constituent file.
location: Clause 5 · Evidence Packaging and Delivery · Offset 232

statement_text: Packaging notes may not replace missing objective evidence and are required to identify any gap that remains open at event closeout.
location: Clause 5 · Evidence Packaging and Delivery · Offset 382

statement_text: Every final verification package shall include an index showing where each supporting artifact can be located in the controlled repository.
location: Clause 5 · Evidence Packaging and Delivery · Offset 532

statement_text: This file follows that pattern as well, which makes it useful for testing that corpus-wide “shall statement” extraction does not explode on references alone.
Verification readiness reviews shall identify every requirement row that still depends on open discrepancies, pending waivers, or incomplete objective evidence.
location: Clause 7 · Review Governance and Audit Readiness · Offset 58

statement_text: Audit packages shall retain the final adjudicated comment history for each disputed trace row.
location: Clause 7 · Review Governance and Audit Readiness · Offset 236

statement_text: Read-ahead material for review boards shall distinguish between accepted evidence, provisional evidence, and planned future evidence.
location: Clause 7 · Review Governance and Audit Readiness · Offset 348

statement_text: The verification control log shall identify when a requirement row moved because of a baseline change rather than because of a verification-method decision.
location: Clause 7 · Review Governance and Audit Readiness · Offset 499

statement_text: Review facilitators are required to preserve attendance, voting, and disposition records for every formal verification readiness decision.
location: Clause 7 · Review Governance and Audit Readiness · Offset 673

statement_text: Matrix exports shall preserve stable row identifiers even when sort order, grouping, or display filters change between releases.
location: Clause 7 · Review Governance and Audit Readiness · Offset 829

statement_text: References to subcontract work statements shall identify the governing task clause rather than a generalized meeting or report label.
References to interface requirements shall preserve the interface clause number and affected boundary description in the trace row narrative.
Imported cybersecurity rows are required to record whether the evidence package came from operational scans, sustainment scans, or accreditation support activities.
When multiple baselines mention the same operational outcome, the matrix shall identify the primary acceptance source and the supporting references separately.
Traceability analysts shall capture any mismatch between source-document terminology and event-procedure terminology before the procedure enters witness review.
